title: Xanthous Springy Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Algerian dish featuring lamb, mixture powder, and a blend of spices, cooked in a kadhai to perfection. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a kadhai over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the mixture powder, brine dry spice mix, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the lemon juice, dried apricots, and chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the kadhai, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the lid and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
